title: DORA Threat-Led Penetration Testing (TLPT) Lifecycle

compliance.assurance_rationale: DORA Art.26 ‚Äî Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that DORA Art.26 (Threat-led penetration testing) is enforced ‚Äî Splunk UC-22.3.17: DORA Threat-Led Penetration Testing (TLPT) Lifecycle.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.3.17' running on index itsm and supporting catalogue-defined data sources, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted ‚Äî SME review required.

dataSources: `dora_tlpt_register.csv` (TLPT engagement tracking), `index=itsm` (remediation tickets), ES Notable events (from purple team exercises)
spl:
| inputlookup dora_tlpt_register.csv
| eval last_tlpt_date=strptime(completion_date, "%Y-%m-%d")
| eval months_since_tlpt=round((now()-last_tlpt_date)/(86400*30), 0)
| eval next_due=if(months_since_tlpt >= 36, "OVERDUE", if(months_since_tlpt >= 30, "DUE_WITHIN_6_MONTHS", "ON_TRACK"))
| eval findings_remediated=if(isnotnull(total_findings) AND isnotnull(findings_closed), round(100*findings_closed/total_findings,1), 0)
| table scope, tester_organization, completion_date, months_since_tlpt, next_due, total_findings, findings_closed, findings_remediated, critical_findings_open
| sort next_due
description: Article 26 requires financial entities identified by competent authorities to conduct TLPT at least every three years, following TIBER-EU methodology with qualified external testers. This use case tracks the TLPT lifecycle ‚Äî from threat intelligence scoping through red team execution to remediation of findings ‚Äî ensuring the advanced testing programme that DORA mandates for systemically important entities is completed and acted upon.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for DORA compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes itsm. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Create `dora_tlpt_register.csv` with TLPT engagement records (scope, tester, completion date, finding counts); (2) track three-year cycle per Art. 26; (3) monitor finding remediation ‚Äî critical findings should be remediated within 90 days; (4) validate tester qualifications per Art. 27; (5) coordinate pooled TLPT with entities sharing the same ICT provider where applicable; (6) report TLPT status to competent authority. Indexes required: `index=itsm`.
visualization: Table (TLPT status), Single value (months since last TLPT), Gauge (finding remediation %), Bar chart (open findings by severity). Key fields: `completion_date`, `critical_findings_open`, `findings_closed`, `findings_remediated`, `last_tlpt_date`, `months_since_tlpt`.
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern ‚Äî correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263).
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `dora_tlpt_register.csv` (TLPT engagement tracking), `index=itsm` (remediation tickets), ES Notable events (from purple team exercises).
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
(1) Create `dora_tlpt_register.csv` with TLPT engagement records (scope, tester, completion date, finding counts); (2) track three-year cycle per Art. 26; (3) monitor finding remediation ‚Äî critical findings should be remediated within 90 days; (4) validate tester qualifications per Art. 27; (5) coordinate pooled TLPT with entities sharing the same ICT provider where applicable; (6) report TLPT status to competent authority.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| inputlookup dora_tlpt_register.csv
| eval last_tlpt_date=strptime(completion_date, "%Y-%m-%d")
| eval months_since_tlpt=round((now()-last_tlpt_date)/(86400*30), 0)
| eval next_due=if(months_since_tlpt >= 36, "OVERDUE", if(months_since_tlpt >= 30, "DUE_WITHIN_6_MONTHS", "ON_TRACK"))
| eval findings_remediated=if(isnotnull(total_findings) AND isnotnull(findings_closed), round(100*findings_closed/total_findings,1), 0)
| table scope, tester_organization, completion_date, months_since_tlpt, next_due, total_findings, findings_closed, findings_remediated, critical_findings_open
| sort next_due
```

Understanding this SPL

**DORA Threat-Led Penetration Testing (TLPT) Lifecycle (Art. 26)** ‚Äî Article 26 requires financial entities identified by competent authorities to conduct TLPT at least every three years, following TIBER-EU methodology with qualified external testers. This use case tracks the TLPT lifecycle ‚Äî from threat intelligence scoping through red team execution to remediation of findings ‚Äî ensuring the advanced testing programme that DORA mandates for systemically important entities is completed and acted upon.

Documented **Data sources**: `dora_tlpt_register.csv` (TLPT engagement tracking), `index=itsm` (remediation tickets), ES Notable events (from purple team exercises). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Loads rows via `inputlookup` (KV store or CSV lookup) for enrichment or reporting.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **last_tlpt_date** ‚Äî applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **months_since_tlpt** ‚Äî applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **next_due** ‚Äî applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **findings_remediated** ‚Äî applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.

‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Output fields: completion_date, critical_findings_open, findings_closed, findings_remediated, last_tlpt_date, months_since_tlpt, next_due, scope.

Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (TLPT status), Single value (months since last TLPT), Gauge (finding remediation %), Bar chart (open findings by severity).
